subject: FW: Urgent Senior Next.js UI Developer Opportunity in Phoenix AZ for Western Alliance Bank.
body:
Hello,

I hope you are doing well! 🙂

We have an urgent requirement and would appreciate it if you could review the job description below. If this opportunity aligns with your skills and experience, please share your updated profile along with the requested details mentioned under the JD.

PV Note: Urgent Next.js Developer Opportunity in in Phoenix, AZ for Western alliance Bank.

Job Description -

Job Title: Senior Next.js UI Developer –F2F

Job Location: Phoenix AZ – 5 days Onsite (Need local with DL )

Client: Western Alliance Bank

Visa: CITIZEN,GC EAD,GREEN CARD,H4 EAD

MOI: Skype+ F2F

 Need LinkedIn

 

Need an experienced Next.js UI Sr. Developer to join our engineering team. The ideal candidate has strong front-end expertise, deep knowledge of React and Next.js, and experience building high-performance, scalable, and secure user interfaces. You will collaborate closely with backend engineers  and designers to deliver modern and responsive applications.
 

Key Responsibilities

*	Develop, maintain, and optimize web applications using Next.js, React, and modern UI frameworks.
*	Build reusable UI components, layouts, and design system-compliant interfaces.
*	Work with REST APIs or internal services and manage client-side/server-side data flows.
*	Use state management solutions such as Zustand, Redux Toolkit, or TanStack Query (React Query).
*	Implement SSR/SSG/ISR strategies for performance and scalability.
*	Ensure responsiveness, accessibility, and cross-browser compatibility.
*	Implement secure authentication and authorization flows (e.g. MSAL, OAuth).
*	Implement client side error logging and monitoring to improve application reliability
*	Collaborate with UX designers to convert wireframes into smooth user experiences.
*	Write unit, integration, UI, and E2E tests using Jest, Testing Library, and Playwright.
*	Follow CI/CD pipelines and deployment workflows (e.g., Azure DevOps or container-based deployments).

 

Required Skills & Experience

*	Strong proficiency in JavaScript, TypeScript, React, and Next.js.
*	Hands-on experience with Next.js App Router, routing, layouts, and data-fetching patterns.
*	Solid understanding of modern UI/CSS frameworks: Tailwind CSS, Coin Base Design System(CDS)
*	Strong understanding of web performance optimization, caching, prefetching, in modern apps
*	Experience with Git, branching strategies, and agile development environments.
*	Knowledge of  web performance and security best practices
*	Strong understanding of error handling, logging, and debugging strategies
*	Experience integrating with .NET backend services or SignalR (real-time updates).

 

Education Required::

•            Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or equivalent practical work experience.

 

Looking forward to your response.

Best Regards,
Shubham Singh
Technical Recruiter
TEK Inspirations LLC
13573 Tabasco Cat Trail, Frisco, TX 75035
📧 Email: shubham@tekinspirations.com <mailto:shubham@tekinspirations.com>  | ☎️ Phone: +1 (469)-498-0636
📱 WhatsApp: +91-953-232-0892 | 💬 G Chat: www.sbmtek@gmail.com <http://www.sbmtek@gmail.com> 

  _____  

Disclaimer: If you are not interested in receiving our emails, please reply with "REMOVE" in the subject line to remove@tekinspirations.com <mailto:remove@tekinspirations.com> . Kindly include all relevant email addresses that should be unsubscribed. We apologize for any inconvenience.

subject: J D : Frontend Developer
body:
Job Description :

 

Title                     : Software Developer

Type                    : Hybrid

Client                  : Vanguard

Location             : Malvern, PA

Duration            : 12+ months

MOI                     : Virtual

 

Responsibilities : 

*	Design, develop, and maintain responsive web applications using
React
*	Translate UX/UI designs and wireframes into high-quality, accessible
user interfaces
*	Build reusable components and front-end libraries for consistency
and scalability
*	Integrate frontend applications with RESTful APIs and backend
services
*	Collaborate with backend engineers using Python-based services
(e.g., APIs, data processing)
*	Optimize applications for performance, scalability, and
cross-browser compatibility
*	Write clean, maintainable, and well-tested code following
development best practices
*	Participate in code reviews and contribute to team-wide frontend
standards
*	Work within Agile / Scrum delivery teams to support iterative
development and release cycles
*	Troubleshoot and debug UI, performance, and integration issues

 

Qualifications : 

*	Experience 6-7 years
*	React Typescript Vite AWS Services (lambda, s3, IAM, ecs, event
bridge)
*	Additionally experience developing micro frontends would be
advantageous
*	Collaborate with backend engineers using Python-based services
(e.g., APIs, data processing)

 

 

Thanks & Regards..*

subject: NWCU Online Law School Application No. 21447
body:
Thank you for your interest in Northwestern California University. This
message is being sent to confirm that we did receive your application for
enrollment in the law study program. Your application number is 21447.

After we receive your official transcripts, it usually takes 2-4 weeks for
the review process to be completed. If you do not hear from us within that
time frame, please check your computer system’s spam or junk folder for an
email message from admissions@nwculaw.edu. If one has not been received,
please call the school at 916-920-9470 to check on the status.

*What transcripts do I need to have sent?*

   1. If you have a bachelor’s degree from a U.S. regionally accredited or
   state-approved college or university; then we need an official* transcript
   from every college or university from which you earned any degree.
   2. If have an associate of arts or associate of science degree from a
   U.S. regionally-accredited or state-approved college or university; then we
   need an official* transcript from every college or university from which
   you earned any degree.
   3. If you do not have a bachelor’s degree, nor an associate (A.A. or
   A.S.) degree, but you have earned at least 60 semester credits or 90
   quarter credits applicable toward a bachelor’s degree from a U.S.
   regionally-accredited or state-approved college or university; then we need
   an official* transcript from each and every U.S. college or university you
   have have ever attended.
   4. If you do not  have a U.S. bachelor's degree, nor associate degree,
   nor at least 60 semester credits or 90 quarter credits applicable toward a
   bachelor’s degree from a U.S. regionally-accredited or state-approved
   college or university, but you do have an equivalent degree or credits from
   a foreign college or university, then we need an official* course-by-course
   evaluation  by an evaluation service approved by the State Bar of
   California showing your degree equivalence, credit equivalence, and
   U.S.-equivalent GPA.
   5. If you have I have none of the above, then we need an official* copy
   of every college or university you have ever attended, plus official* CLEP
   score reports showing that you obtained a passing score on the College
   Composition CLEP exam, plus passing scores on: (a) two additional CLEP
   exams each of which is recommended for at least 6 credits; or (b) four
   additional CLEP exams each of which is recommended for at least 3 units; or
   (c) three additional CLEP exams, one of which is of which is recommended
   for at least 6 units and two of which are recommended for at least 3 units.

"Official" means that the transcript is either: (1) an official, hard-copy
transcript, foreign degree evaluation, or CLEP score report which arrives
at the NWCU office unopened in its original envelope from the issuing
institution, or (2) an official electronic transcript sent to
transcripts@nwculaw.edu only by Parchment or National Student
Clearinghouse. Emails sent to our transcripts email address by others are
not opened.

*Where do I send my transcripts, evaluations, or CLEP Score reports?*

Official transcripts may be sent to us in one of two ways:

*(1) Hard-copy, official transcripts*: An official transcript is one that
arrives at our office sealed in the original envelope from the sending
institution. If the envelope has been opened or appears tampered, then the
transcript is no longer official. You may arrange for the transcript to be
mailed or delivered to our office at:

Northwestern California University School of Law

2151 River Plaza Drive, Suite 306

Sacramento, California 95833



*(2) Electronic, official transcripts:* Official electronic transcripts are
accepted only from Parchment or National Student Clearinghouse. The
transcript must be emailed directly to the following email address:

transcripts@nwculaw.edu.


Please note that the "transcripts" email address is for official
transcripts only; emails sent to it will not be opened and will not receive
a response.

Foreign degree evaluations are not accepted electronically. A
hard-copy official evaluation must be mailed to the school office. Note
that a course-by-course evaluation showing degree equivalence, credit
equivalence, and GPA equivalence is required. For a list of approved
evaluation services, please check the State Bar of California website.

CLEP score reports are not accepted electronically. A
hard-copy official score report must be mailed to the school office.

*What is the school's office address for mailing of official transcripts,
evaluations, or CLEP score reports?*

Northwestern California University School of Law

2151 River Plaza Drive, Suite 306

Sacramento, California 95833



*I have previously applied or previously attended. Do I still need to send
transcripts, evaluations, or CLEP score reports?*



Usually yes.If you applied within the last 12 months, we may have the
necessary records if you sent them previously. If you attended less than 12
months ago, we should have any records you sent previously. In either of
these cases, please allow 2-4 weeks for retrieval of your prior records and
review of your current application.If you do not hear from us within that
time frame, please check your computer system’s spam or junk folder for an
email message from admissions@nwculaw.edu. If one has not been received,
please call the school at 916-920-9470 to check on the status.

Day-to-day maintenance and operations for Safety office app using React / Node / Postgres.

 

Description:

Seeking a Full Stack Sr. Ops Engineer to support the Safety Office portfolio of applications. Having 6+ years of demonstrable full stack development experience in React JS and Node JS together with working knowledge of PostgreSQL, Oracle, Ci/CD, version control, microservices would be key for individual’ success in a fast paced / agile environment. In this Ops. role, individual would bring their A-game to interface with user community, implement end-user requirements adopting industry best SDLC practices and be responsible for day-to-day application monitoring-triage-issue resolution.

In addition to core technical skills, individual should have strong verbal / written communication skills, be curious, open minded, have eye for both detail and quality, be team player and willing to do whatever it takes to deliver on time with minimal guidance.

Top skills

PostgreSQL – strong SQL skills, writing queries, performance tuning

React – HTML5, CSS, JavaScript

NodeJS

Rest APIs, Microservices

Python – scripting, data processing, automation

Containerization – Podman, Docker (for runner and managing app containers)

Version Control using Git

CI/CD

LINUX (RHEL) - troubleshooting, basic server operations

Day-to-day maintenance and operations for Safety office app using React /

Seeking a Full Stack Sr. Ops Engineer to support the Safety Office portfolio
of applications. Having 6+ years of demonstrable full stack development
experience in React JS and Node JS together with working knowledge of
PostgreSQL, Oracle, Ci/CD, version control, microservices would be key for
individual' success in a fast paced / agile environment. In this Ops. role,
individual would bring their A-game to interface with user community,
implement end-user requirements adopting industry best SDLC practices and be
responsible for day-to-day application monitoring-triage-issue resolution.

written communication skills, be curious, open minded, have eye for both
detail and quality, be team player and willing to do whatever it takes to
deliver on time with minimal guidance.

*	NodeJS

containers)

subject: UI Developer | Location: Onsite in Milwaukee, WI
body:
Please share below Submittal details..

 

Current Location:-

DOB:-

Last 4 Digit SSN:-

LinkedIn:-

Education Details:-

Driving License Copy:-

Work authorization:-

 

 

Job Description -

UI Developer
Location: Onsite in Milwaukee, WI

candidates must be able to do 4 days onsite in Milwaukee, WI and the client
will not accept candidates that would have to relocate for the role. 

VISA GC and USC should be genuine 

Role Overview

We are seeking a UI Developer with 1-3 years of experience who is passionate
about user-interface design and creating intuitive, high-quality frontend
experiences. This role goes far beyond coding - we're looking for someone
who enjoys the design process, cares deeply about visual quality, and wants
to build elegant, mobile-friendly applications used by internal stakeholders
across the organization.

You will contribute to UI development across a suite of 11 production
applications, all of which have mobile components (primarily iOS). Many
applications share reusable backend components, with customized workflows
layered on top. The team is also beginning to embed AI-powered features
(e.g., ESG, analytics, workflow intelligence).

Key Responsibilities

*	Build modern, user-friendly UI components for internal business
applications.
*	Partner with Business Analysts, Designers, and Developers to design
and implement new features.
*	Contribute to UI/UX planning, wireframing, and visual design
discussions.
*	Develop responsive interfaces for both desktop and mobile
environments.
*	Maintain consistency with internal brand guidelines and custom UI
frameworks.
*	Participate in the full development lifecycle within an agile team
structure.
*	Support ongoing enhancements, maintenance efforts, and feature
modernization.
*	Ensure a high standard of visual quality - addressing a key
improvement area for the team.

Required Skills

*	Intermediate JavaScript development skills.
*	Experience with React (primary UI library) and Redux (state
management).
*	Familiarity with Bootstrap, Tailwind, or similar design systems.
*	Ability to work within or contribute to custom component libraries.
*	Understanding of UI/UX principles and visual design fundamentals.
*	Bonus: Exposure to Angular, Vue, or React Native.

 

Best Regards,

Anand Singh

| IT Recruiter | TEK Inspirations LLC |
W: (469) 638-7409 

 <mailto:anand.singh@tekinspirations.com> anand.singh@tekinspirations.com

subject: Rate confirmation
body:
Hi Nina
 It was nice talking with you.!
 As we discussed kindly confirm the rate $55/hr. on W2 (Without Benefits) for the position Front-End Developer (React.js), San Leandro, CA with the direct client of  Innova Solutions

Job Description

  *   Job Role: Front-End Developer (React.js)
  *   Location: San Leandro, CA (Onsite / Hybrid as required)
  *   Local Candidates Only
  *   Employment Type: Contract
  *   Category: Technology | Software Engineering | Front-End Development
 Role Overview
Team is seeking an experienced Front-End Developer to support application modernization initiatives. This role focuses on enhancing existing frontend codebases and collaborating closely with an Agile development team to deliver scalable, high-quality user interfaces for enterprise applications.

  *   The ideal candidate brings strong React.js expertise, solid JavaScript fundamentals, and experience working in large-scale enterprise environments.
 Key Responsibilities

  *   Enhance and modernize existing frontend applications using React.js
  *   Collaborate with cross-functional Agile teams, including backend engineers, QA, UX designers, and product owners
  *   Develop reusable, maintainable, and high-performance UI components
  *   Work with Redux and React Hooks for state management and application logic
  *   Review, refactor, and optimize existing code to improve performance and maintainability
  *   Participate in Agile ceremonies (stand-ups, sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives)
  *   Follow enterprise coding standards, best practices, and security guidelines
  *   Contribute to technical documentation and knowledge sharing within the team
 Required Qualifications

  *   5-7 years of Front-End Development experience
  *   2-3 years of hands-on React.js experience
 Strong proficiency in:

  *   JavaScript (ES6+)
  *   React.js
  *   Redux
  *   React Hooks
  *   Experience working in enterprise-scale environments
  *   Strong understanding of frontend architecture, component-based design, and modern UI development practices
  *   Experience working in Agile / Scrum development teams
  *   Excellent problem-solving, communication, and collaboration skills
 Preferred / Nice to Have

  *   Experience working at large enterprise organizations (e.g., Apple, Meta, Intuit, JPMC, Federal Reserve Bank, or similar)
  *   Experience modernizing or refactoring legacy frontend applications
  *   Familiarity with frontend testing frameworks (Jest, React Testing Library)
  *   Exposure to CI/CD pipelines and version control systems (Git)
  *   Note: Candidates with prior Wells Fargo experience are not preferred for this role.






Thanks & Regards

Solai Rajan
Sr. Technical Recruiter

subject: Password change for your Yahoo account
body:
Hi Shuzi,

The password for your Yahoo account, g*****18, was recently changed.

If you made this change, you're all set.

Didn’t change your password?

Please take these steps to secure your account.

  1. Recover your account here:

  2. Review your phone numbers and email addresses and remove the ones that don’t belong to you.
    3. https://login.yahoo.com/account/security

Thanks,

Yahoo

subject: New sign in on Thunderbird Mail
body:
Hi Shuzi,

Your Yahoo account galiana18 was used to sign in to a new third party application on Thunderbird Mail.

If this wasn’t you, please use this link to revoke third party access to your account, and change your password. You can also revoke access to Thunderbird Mail by going to yahoo.com and navigating to the Recent Activity tab under Account Info.

If this was you, you’re all set.

We recommend using the Yahoo Mail app to access galiana18 or your other email accounts including Gmail, Outlook, or AOL. Download the free Yahoo Mail app here or by visiting the App Store.

Thanks,

Yahoo
